completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Added deterministic_core doctest output normalization and snapshot matching (no AI review, cache, mutation, or broad CLI wiring -- all non-goals). src/doctest/normalizer.zig is pure and idempotent: a single scan-and-replace driver rewrites absolute project paths to <project>, OS temp dirs (/tmp, /var/folders, /private/var/folders) to <tmp>, generated workspace hashes under .zig-cache/zentinel/doctest to <workspace>, doctest_run_<ulid> run ids to a stable placeholder, ISO-8601 timestamps to <timestamp>, durations (NNms/NNus/NNns/NNs, optional fraction) to <duration>, strips ANSI CSI color sequences, canonicalizes CRLF/CR to LF, and trims trailing whitespace per line while preserving line order; re-normalizing is a no-op. src/doctest/matcher.zig implements the seven match modes: exact (trailing-trimmed equality), contains (expected lines must appear in order), regex (a pure backtracking engine supporting literals, ., character classes/ranges/negation, \d/\w/\s, *, +, ?, and ^/$ anchors -- no external dependency), diagnostic (collapse :line:col references then contains), and json/json_subset/json_unordered (parse both sides with std.json.parseFromSliceLeaky and compare semantically so object key order never matters; subset allows extra actual object keys and a positional array prefix; unordered treats arrays as multisets). src/doctest/snapshot.zig orchestrates: it normalizes the actual output, normalizes the expected block (except a regex pattern, which is left raw), matches under the requested mode, and on mismatch emits a ZNTL_DOCTEST_SNAPSHOT_MISMATCH diagnostic carrying file, line, durable case id, and bounded expected/actual evidence; matchResultOutput compares a runner.CaseResult's stdout; renderDiagnostic produces a deterministic multi-line mismatch report; snapshot updates remain manual. completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Added deterministic doctest case extraction (deterministic_core; no command or compiler execution, no case mutation, no snapshot matching). src/doctest/case.zig defines the canonical CaseKind enum (zig_compile_pass, zig_test, zig_compile_fail, cli, config, config_fail, mutation), the typed Case struct (durable id, file, kind, label, anchor source_ref, secondary block_refs, line_start/line_end/anchor_line kept separate from the id), and the durable `dt_...` id derivation: `dt_ + first_26(lowercase_unpadded_crockford_base32(sha256(canonical_bytes)))` over namespace `zentinel.doctest_case.v1`, the project-relative doc path, case kind, explicit label ("" when absent), normalized block grouping metadata, and a hex content hash of the grouped blocks -- line numbers are deliberately excluded so unrelated prose never renumbers cases (same base32 packing discipline as src/mutant.zig). src/doctest/extractor.zig groups parser blocks deterministically: a producer block (zig test -> zig_test, zig compile_fail -> zig_compile_fail, bash cli -> cli, toml config -> config, zig before -> mutation, plain zig -> zig_compile_pass) opens a case; following expectation blocks (text output / json expected) and a `zig after` for a `before` attach as secondary block_refs; a documentation-only block ends implicit grouping. Invalid groupings emit ZNTL_DOCTEST_INVALID_BLOCK diagnostics (orphan expectation with no producer, `zig after` without a preceding `zig before`, `zig before` without a matching `zig after`, an expected-output block on a compile-pass producer, and duplicate unlabeled identical cases -- detected by durable-id collision and rejected as ambiguous rather than receiving occurrence-based ids). Output is sorted by file, anchor line, block start, then id, and extractor.renderInventory serializes a deterministic JSON inventory (cases + diagnostics) for future reports. extractSource parses then extracts and folds parser diagnostics in. completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Added src/mutators/loop_boundary.zig (deterministic_core): the stable loop_boundary mutator, completing the twelve minimum-product mutators. For while loops it reads the condition via the while node data (while_simple -> node_and_node[0]; while_cont and @"while" -> node_and_extra[0]) and, when the condition is a boundary comparison (<, <=, >, >=), swaps the inclusive/exclusive boundary (< <-> <=, > <-> >=) with compiles compile expectation (the swap always type-checks). For for ranges it reads the range end (for_range node_and_opt_node[1], skipping open ranges a..) and, when the end is a plain decimal integer literal, emits paired +1/-1 candidates (i128 math, replacement strings via the collector allocator) with may_fail compile expectation (the bound can overflow the range type or form an invalid range). Infinite loops (while true: the condition is the `true` identifier, not a comparison), non-comparison conditions, open or non-integer range ends, and test bodies are skipped. Candidate order is deterministic via the shared collector's canonical sort. Equality comparisons (==, !=) in while conditions are NOT boundary-swapped (boundary is inclusive/exclusive only). No ast_backend or mutant model change was needed. Re-exported via zentinel.mutators.loop_boundary; run-pipeline enabling and overlap handling with comparison_boundary for loop conditions remain deferred (run_command.zig out of scope) and reports need no schema change.

completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Added src/mutators/integer_boundary.zig (deterministic_core): the stable integer_literal_boundary mutator. It walks comparison nodes (==, !=, <, <=, >, >=) and, for each operand that is a `.number_literal`, emits two boundary candidates: the literal incremented by one and decremented by one (parsed as i128 to avoid overflow when computing +/-1; replacement strings allocated from the collector allocator). Context is an allowlist: only literals that are direct comparison operands are mutated, so this covers branch and length-check boundaries while protected literals -- version numbers, ABI/mask constants, enum tags, array lengths, and align(...) values -- are never examined because they are not comparison operands. Only plain decimal literals are mutated; hex/binary/octal and underscore/suffix forms are skipped as syntax-local (type inference out of scope). Test bodies are excluded. Compile expectation is may_fail because a +/-1 can overflow the operand's integer type (a documented expected compile error, not an invalid candidate). completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Implemented `zentinel run` for the single-threaded Phase 1 flow. src/project_model.zig (deterministic_core) provides pure glob matching (`*` within a segment, `**` across segments), include/exclude eligibility, and deterministic source discovery over a directory (sorted, forward-slashed project-relative paths) so the mutated file set is derived from config globs, not a hardcoded list. src/run_command.zig (deterministic_core) orchestrates the flow over injected providers (a baseline runner.Executor and a per-mutant MutantRunner) and pure source bytes: it rejects run.jobs>1 (error.JobsNotSupported) and --output outside the project root (error.OutputOutsideRoot) up front, runs the baseline, on baseline failure emits run.status=baseline_failed with structured baseline command evidence and zero counts (exit 3, covering both non-zero failure and timeout->failed), otherwise generates Phase 1 AST candidates (arithmetic/comparison/logical/boolean) over the discovered files, filters by config mutators_enabled plus --operator/--mutant, runs each mutant through the injected MutantRunner, assembles report mutants (line-level diff, mutant-phase command evidence, all/strategy test selection), assigns canonical display ids, and derives summary counts. --fail-on-survivors changes only the exit code (1), never report fields. parseArgs is the pure run-option parser (rejects unknown options/missing values/invalid --report). src/cli.zig (presentation_adapter) wires the real providers: it loads config, validates Zig (non-fatal), discovers and reads eligible files, runs the real baseline via std.process.run (direct argv, no shell, configured timeout via Io.Timeout), and runs each mutant in an isolated per-mutant workspace under .zig-cache/zentinel/workspaces/<run>/<mutant-id>/ (copy the project minus caches/VCS, write the patched file, run with cwd=workspace, then delete the workspace), so the developer working tree is never modified (docs/SANDBOX_SECURITY.md). The recorded cwd is a stable label, never the raw workspace path. The JSON report is written under the project root (--output is root-restricted) and a concise survivor-focused summary is printed. No parallelism, cache, AI, or experimental backends (task non-goals).
